prevalence.trend: Stable to increasing due to aging population

burden.trend: Decreasing slightly due to tobacco control

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: GOLD guidelines: Post-bronchodilator FEV1/FVC <0.70 via spirometry; symptoms of dyspnea, cough, sputum; exclusion of other causes

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Low (<20% routine spirometry in primary care)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Follows international GOLD standards, spirometry recommended but underutilized
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: SUS provides bronchodilators (SABA/LABA), ICS for exacerbations, smoking cessation programs, pulmonary rehab; oxygen therapy available in select centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High underdiagnosis in rural/North regions due to access; biomass smoke in Amazon/Northeast; urban pollution in Southeast; inequities in SUS coverage
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National tobacco control (CEXTAS program reducing smoking prevalence to ~12%); SBPT guidelines; growing telemedicine for respiratory care
